title: Stacees Non-Amazon Scrape - 2026-05-17

metadata.reason: Desktop thread full transcript export was not available; this focused file captures the Stacees scrape request, implementation, outputs, and handoff updates for durable project memory.

next_steps: Use the Stacees raw JSONL sidecar when review_id, review_url, rating, ordered size, and image_source_type are needed beyond the standard intake CSV fields.
For future Stacees refreshes, use the canonical /sitemap/sitemap_product.xml.gz product list and the public https://api-nl.stacees.com review endpoints.
Do not use aggregate rating or review summaries as scrape data; collect /comment/ajaxreviews row payloads after probing /api/appCommentConditions per SKU.

messages.text: Asked to scrape stacees.com as a first-time non-Amazon source for FWM, check scrape rules and prior claims/outputs first, create a claim, inspect product pages and public review endpoints carefully because the triage URL might have a bad reviews anchor, cover every public product on the website, collect actual review rows and customer review images with product URL/title, review ID/URL, text, reviewer info, rating/date, ordered size, body/measurement data, and image_source_type, and stop on 429/captcha/WAF/auth blocks with a clear blocked/revisit note.

Checked new-scrape-rules.md, the non-Amazon scrape triage plan, prior Stacees mentions, existing active/completed claims, and existing output directories. Created active Stacees claim files in both the repo and FWM_Data claim areas before scraping.

Inspected stacees.com robots.txt and sitemap.xml. The site allows crawling with Crawl-delay 10 and exposes a canonical product sitemap at https://www.stacees.com/sitemap/sitemap_product.xml.gz. Direct product pages are Nuxt SSR pages with a misspelled #porductsReviewsSort reviews anchor and aggregate rating data that was not used as row data.

Mined Stacees JavaScript chunks and found public review endpoints: /comment/ajaxreviews, /comment/reviewPicture, /comment/getreviewinfo, /api/appCommentConditions, plus the gallery endpoints /gallery/index and /gallery/details. Resolved the API base as https://api-nl.stacees.com after same-host endpoint probes returned Nuxt 404 HTML.

Verified actual public review rows by using /api/appCommentConditions for a SKU to discover size/color/grade filters, then querying /comment/ajaxreviews with web_sku, base_sku, and filter values. Confirmed this returns row-level fields including review id, title, content, username, grade, add_time, ordered size, color, fit, gallery id, and thumb_list customer images.

Implemented data-pipelines/non-amazon/scripts/step_1_raw_scrape/scrape_stacees_reviews.py. The scraper uses the canonical product sitemap, checks every public product SKU through /api/appCommentConditions, calls /comment/ajaxreviews for public review filters, stops on configured pressure/status/challenge markers, writes the standard FWM intake CSV, writes a raw JSONL sidecar with review URLs and row-level details, and writes a summary JSON.

Ran a smoke test, fixed a ReviewImage dataclass argument bug, tightened product discovery to the canonical sitemap instead of language variants, added transient timeout retries while preserving hard stop behavior for 429/captcha/WAF/auth-like responses, and then completed the full scrape.

Final Stacees output: 3,767 canonical public products discovered and checked; 575 products had review filters; 518 products produced customer review image rows; 2,142 raw and deduped intake rows; 1,135 unique reviews; 365 unique product URLs represented in rows; 2,142 rows with customer image, ordered size, rating, and review URL; 21 rows with at least one measurement field; 21 Supabase-qualified rows. No 429, captcha, WAF, or auth block was encountered.

Wrote outputs under /Users/briannasinger/Projects/FWM_Data/non-amazon/data/step_1_raw_scraping_data/stacees_com: stacees_com_reviews_matching_intake_schema.csv, stacees_com_review_rows_raw.jsonl, and stacees_com_reviews_matching_intake_schema_summary.json. Moved the Stacees claim to /Users/briannasinger/Projects/FWM_Data/non-amazon/data/step_1_raw_scraping_data/_claims/stacees_com_2026-05-17_completed.claim and removed the active Stacees claims.
Asked how many Supabase-qualified rows were in the Stacees scrape.
Counted rows using the local Supabase qualification rule of image URL plus product URL plus size plus at least one measurement field, and answered that Stacees has 21 Supabase-qualified rows.
Asked to update the remote Git, S3, and the transcripts table.

Prepared this focused transcript for upload to the Supabase codex_chat_transcripts table, planned to sync /Users/briannasinger/Projects/FWM_Data to the configured S3 bucket, and planned to commit only the Stacees scraper and transcript while leaving unrelated worktree changes from other scrape threads untouched.
